title: "The King in Yellow" Minecraft ARG
about: "The King in Yellow" Minecraft ARG refers to a character inspired by a 1895 "weird-horror" anthology book by Robert W. Chambers, in which a fictional play featuring an entity referred to as "the Stranger" and a "Yellow Sign" drives readers mad. The "King in Yellow" experienced a resurgence in popular culture in the 2020s, following a series of horror-themed videos that adapted the character. Notably, YouTuber Wifies posted a Minecraft themed horror ARG (alternate reality game) titled "Searching For A World That Doesn’t Exist," where a player named Avery encounters the King in Yellow. The video inspired fan art and numerous fan-made TikToks in November 2025.
origin:
The King in Yellow is the title of a collection of weird-horror short stories by Robert W. Chambers, first published in 1895.[1] The first four stories are connected by a cursed fictional play named "The King in Yellow," and feature themes like "Carcosa" and a "Yellow" sign that drives readers mad. The King in Yellow has been repeatedly referenced in pop culture, with HBO's True Detective using "Carcosa," the "Yellow King" and ritualistic yellow symbology as metaphors for cosmic evil and corruption.[2]

On April 22nd, 2025, YouTuber[4] Wendigood posted a video titled, "The Story that Kills You – The King in Yellow," recounting the 1895 book and its themes in a video that gathered over 4 million plays in seven months.

On October 18th, 2025, YouTuber[8] AverytheMayo posted a video about how he discovered a mine in a Minecraft game he claims to have found on a laptop he discovered in a storage locker. The video shows him finding a book that instructs him, "Don't turn left," possibly to save him from meeting the King in Yellow. The video gathered over 900,000 plays in a month.

On October 24th, 2025, YouTuber[5] Wifies posted a Minecraft ARG (alternate reality game) video inspired by the aforementioned clip, titled, "Searching For A World That Doesn’t Exist." The nearly 40-minute video followed a character named Avery as he "finds a tunnel leading to a Minecraft world that shouldn't exist," and encounters a scary creature who repeatedly outwits him. The video gathered over 15 million plays in a month.
